notes: Structural placeholder seeded at Author. rm-storyboard is a planning skill — it writes the per-beat Remotion build contract (03-storyboard.md), NO React and NO render. Charter refines these into graded dimensions (contract-completeness, layer-layout-validity, facts-verbatim); no media-judge here (rendered-frame quality is graded downstream by rm-render). Do not hand-code regex test logic.

expectations.desc: Writes artifacts/<project>/03-storyboard.md (and nothing else) — markdown only, no React authored and nothing rendered.

Exactly one beat row per script beat, in order; every row names block(s)/component, structure + transition out (or '— (final)' on the last beat), a layer layout (base Sequence + overlays as nested Sequences), and a frame range in BOTH seconds and frames.

A Composition header records the <Composition> id + root size per AR (Studio-16x9/-9x16/-1x1), FPS 30, durationSeconds/durationInFrames (accounting for transition overlap), and the palette hex + fonts + seed taken from 01-concept.md; multiple ARs get a separate header+layout each.

On-screen text and data figures in each row are copied verbatim from 02-script.md (no VO/narration lines, no invented copy, no rounded-away numbers); uses Remotion layer vocabulary (nested <Sequence>), never HyperFrames 'data-track-index'.
